mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and stable consumer price indices indicate robust demand without inflationary pressure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of triggered cases over a 2-year rolling backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices signal expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during early recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during early recovery periods, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and wage growth, consistently generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220, inflationary pressures force monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 220 coincide with positive forward returns in greater than 50 percent of observations over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and real gross domestic product confirms synchronized demand expansion across residential and aggregate sectors, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST and GDPC1 signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 60-day horizons in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising unemployment indicates supply-side contraction and labor market deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during late-cycle downturns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal predicts positive returns in greater than 55 percent of late-cycle downturn instances over a 2-year sample.
